2606.24047.title: Ensemble Feature Selection and Harris Hawks Optimization for Explainable Mental Health Risk Prediction in Female Sex Workers
2606.24047.paper_summary: One of the significant mental health issues affecting female sex workers (FSWs) is mental disorders, especially depression. Exposure to violence, stigma, and economic hardship further increases their psychological risk. Current machine learning (ML) models are typically ineffective at capturing the high-dimensional and complex risk patterns that exist in this marginalized group. This paper suggests a hybrid predictive model that merges an ensemble feature selection strategy using ANOVA and mutual information and Harris Hawks optimization-tuned logistic regression and represents a new application of swarm intelligence to predict mental health in vulnerable groups. The explainable AI (XAI) methods can be used to understand the factors of trauma associated with model predictions. When applied to a group of 3,005 FSWs, it can be seen that the proposed model is more effective than traditional classifiers, with an accuracy of 95.78%, an F1 score of 95.77%, and an AUC of 0.96, and identifying post-traumatic stress, client-related violence, and occupational factors as major contributors to depression. This work bridges the gaps between conventional and ML approaches to develop an XAI tool that enables vulnerable groups to receive early assistance, evidence-based targeted psychosocial care, and health planning.
2606.24047.paper_summary_zh: 女性性工作者（FSWs）所面臨的一個重要心理健康問題是心理障礙，特別是憂鬱症。暴力、污名化和經濟困難的影響進一步增加了她們的心理風險。當前的機器學習（ML）模型通常無法有效捕捉這個邊緣化群體中存在的高維度和複雜風險模式。本文提出了一種混合預測模型，結合了使用ANOVA和互信息的集成特徵選擇策略以及經哈里斯獵鷹優化調整的邏輯回歸，並代表了群體智慧在預測脆弱群體心理健康方面的新應用。可解釋的人工智慧（XAI）方法可以用來理解與模型預測相關的創傷因素。當應用於3,005名FSWs時，可以看出所提出的模型比傳統分類器更有效，準確率為95.78%，F1分數為95.77%，AUC為0.96，並將創傷後壓力、與客戶相關的暴力和職業因素識別為憂鬱症的主要貢獻因素。這項工作填補了傳統方法與機器學習方法之間的空白，開發出一種XAI工具，使脆弱群體能夠獲得早期援助、基於證據的針對性心理社會護理和健康規劃。
2606.24047.author: Ahnaf Atef Choudhury et.al.
2606.24047.authors: Ahnaf Atef Choudhury, Md. Parvej Hoque Palash, Shahriar Siddique Ayon, Ramkrishna Saha, Abdullah Al Mamun
